2604.18419.title: Knowing When to Quit: A Principled Framework for Dynamic Abstention in LLM Reasoning
2604.18419.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) using chain-of-thought reasoning often waste substantial compute by producing long, incorrect responses. Abstention can mitigate this by withholding outputs unlikely to be correct. While most abstention methods decide to withhold outputs before or after generation, dynamic mid-generation abstention considers early termination of unpromising reasoning traces at each token position. Prior work has explored empirical variants of this idea, but principled guidance for the abstention rule remains lacking. We present a formal analysis of dynamic abstention for LLMs, modeling abstention as an explicit action within a regularized reinforcement learning framework. An abstention reward parameter controls the trade-off between compute and information. We show that abstaining when the value function falls below this reward strictly outperforms natural baselines under general conditions. We further derive a principled and efficient method to approximate the value function. Empirical results on mathematical reasoning and toxicity avoidance tasks support our theory and demonstrate improved selective accuracy over existing methods.
2604.18419.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型 (LLMs) 使用連鎖思考推理時，經常因產生冗長且不正確的回應而浪費大量計算資源。避免輸出可以透過不產生不太可能正確的輸出來減輕這一問題。雖然大多數避免輸出的方法在生成之前或之後決定是否要隱藏輸出，但動態中途避免輸出考慮在每個標記位置提前終止不具前景的推理過程。先前的研究探討了這一思想的實證變體，但對於避免輸出的規則仍然缺乏原則性指導。我們提出了對 LLMs 的動態避免輸出的正式分析，將避免輸出建模為一種在正則化強化學習框架內的明確行動。一個避免輸出的獎勵參數控制計算與信息之間的權衡。我們表明，當價值函數低於這一獎勵時進行避免輸出，在一般條件下明顯優於自然基線。我們進一步推導出一種原則性且高效的方法來近似價值函數。在數學推理和毒性避免任務上的實證結果支持我們的理論，並顯示出相較於現有方法的選擇性準確性有所提升。
2604.18419.author: Hen Davidov et.al.
2604.18419.authors: Hen Davidov, Nachshon Cohen, Oren Kalinsky, Yaron Fairstein, Guy Kushilevitz, Ram Yazdi, Patrick Rebeschini
